data.title: Ethereum co-founder Vitalik Buterin advocates for improved DAOs due to their inefficiency and vulnerability, proposing ZK-proof solutions.
data.description: Buterin stated that Decentralized Autonomous Organizations (DAOs) are presently not operating efficiently and are at risk of being overtaken, advocating for remedies utilizing ZK-proofs.

data.title: OpenLedger and Theoriq collaborate to introduce verifiable AI agents in live DeFi markets to address challenges.

data.title: BE OPEN Art names Artist of the Year 2025, awards Community Support Fund grantees. Located in LUGANO, Switzerland.
data.description: In Lugano, Switzerland on January 19, 2026, The online art gallery BE OPEN Art, founded by Elena Baturina's BE OPEN think tank, has revealed the recipient of the Artist of the Year award for 2025. This initiative seeks to highlight promising young artists in the industry. Additionally, the gallery has achieved a number of other notable accomplishments.

data.title: Novelis Inc. appoints Emilio Braghi as COO after 27 years with the company. Leading aluminum solutions provider.
data.description: On January 19, 2026, Novelis Inc., a prominent provider of sustainable aluminum solutions and the global leader in aluminum rolling and recycling, revealed the appointment of Emilio Braghi as Chief Operating Officer (COO). Braghi, a seasoned professional who joined the company in 1999, will now be responsible for overseeing operations.

data.title: SouthWorth Capital Management acquires American Furniture Manufacturing, combining expertise and 45 years of experience.
data.description: SouthWorth Capital Management, a family office based in Memphis, has revealed the acquisition of American Furniture Manufacturing (AFM) and its affiliated companies. This move combines SouthWorth's operational knowledge with AFM's extensive 45+ years of producing upholstered residential furniture for the US market. AFM is situated in Ecru, Mississippi, and operates as a vertically integrated manufacturer.

data.title: Naturmega®'s Peer-Reviewed Study on Ruby-O® Balance shows stronger triglyceride responder outcomes with lower omega-3 dose. #Omega3 #CardiometabolicHealth
data.description: A recently published study in Barranquilla, Colombia explores the impact of omega-3 delivery methods on triglyceride levels and omega-3 absorption. This peer-reviewed research analyzes how different delivery architectures affect these outcomes, shedding light on their role in cardiometabolic health. The study provides valuable insights into the relationship between omega-3 intake and its incorporation in the body. This research contributes to the understanding of how omega-3 consumption can influence triglyceride levels and overall cardiometabolic well-being.

data.title: Dominion Dynamics raises $21M CAD in seed round led by Georgian to build defense systems, shift in national security.
data.description: Dominion Dynamics, a defense technology company, announced a pre-seed raise after launching in Q4 of 2025. The company's accelerated financing signifies a significant change in Canadian national security. Based in Ottawa, ON, the company is focused on engineering interoperable and attritable systems for contested theatres. The funding raise is set to impact the industry, showcasing a generational shift.

data.title: Crypto market opens lower with Bitcoin peaking at $95,000, leading to $874.01 million liquidations. Bitcoin ETFs see $394.7 million outflows, Ethereum ETFs record $4.64 million inflows. Bitcoin at $92,939.91 and Ethereum at $3,215.72.
data.description: The price of Bitcoin dropped on Monday after reaching close to $95,000, leading to significant deleveraging in the cryptocurrency market. Liquidations totaled $874.01 million in the past 24 hours. Bitcoin ETFs experienced net outflows of $394.7 million while Ethereum ETFs saw net inflows of $4.64 million. Current cryptocurrency prices are: Bitcoin - $92,939.91, Ethereum - $3,215.72, Solana - $133.63, XRP - $1.97. Despite these fluctuations, there have not been any significant movements. For more details, visit the full article on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Financial tech provider Plugsic launches "Delta-Zero" risk protocol, integrating hedging mechanics into retail accounts.
data.description: Financial technology infrastructure provider Plugsic revealed on January 19, 2026, the launch of its innovative "Delta-Zero" risk management protocol within the "Sovereign Execution" platform. This new feature includes an automated hedging layer to enhance risk management. The update is set to significantly impact the financial industry by providing advanced risk mitigation strategies.

data.title: Outform Group launches Hospitality Division to enhance guest experiences with integrated services. (Characters: 97)
data.description: Outform Group, Inc. in Chicago introduced an integrated service model on January 19, 2026, which merges design, procurement, and technology for hospitality brands. This model aims to enhance operational speed while providing clear cost estimates and maintaining global consistency. The company, along with its subsidiaries, offers this award-winning service to assist brands in the industry.

data.title: Global stocks slide as gold hits record high due to Trump's Greenland tariffs, Euro STOXX 50 down 1.3% with Germany's DAX and France's CAC 40 sliding 1-1.5%. Luxury companies like LVMH drop nearly 4% amid the fallout.
data.description: Global stock markets experienced a significant downtrend on Monday as gold prices hit record highs due to escalating trade tensions caused by President Donald Trump's new tariff threats against Europe. European equities were heavily impacted, with the Euro STOXX 50 index, representing the top 50 eurozone companies, dropping by 1.3%. Major European markets such as Germany's DAX fell by approximately 1%, the French CAC 40 declined by 1.5%, and the UK's FTSE 100 saw a 0.5% decrease. Key European companies faced notable losses, with LVMH seeing a 4% decline, ASML Holding N.V. dropping by 3.6%, SAP SE falling by 2.4%, Hermès International facing a dip, and Novo Nordisk... (for more details, visit Benzinga.com)

data.title: Kingston Technology rises to #28 on Forbes' list of America's Top Private Companies for 2025, maintaining position as top tech company.
data.description: In Fountain Valley, California, Kingston Technology Company, Inc., a renowned provider of memory products and technology solutions, has made significant progress by securing the 28th position on Forbes' prestigious list of America's Top Private Companies for 2025. This achievement highlights Kingston's consistent growth within a dynamic industry. Kingston proudly maintains its position as the leading company in the "Technology Hardware & Equipment" category on the list, a testament to its leadership in the sector. With over twenty years of experience, Kingston continues to demonstrate its expertise and innovation in the market.

data.title: U.S. Bancorp (NYSE: USB) to release Q1 earnings on 2026-01-20, expected EPS of $1.19. Investors eagerly await results.
data.description: U.S. Bancorp (NYSE: USB) is set to announce its quarterly earnings on Tuesday, January 20, 2026. Analysts are predicting an earnings per share (EPS) of $1.19 for the report. Investors are eagerly awaiting the announcement, hoping for results that exceed expectations and positive guidance for the upcoming quarter. It is important for new investors to understand the significance of guidance in assessing a company's performance and future prospects. For more details, the complete article can be found on Benzinga.com.
